_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-25 Order of Disciplinary Action Appeal (Revised 07/10/2025, MDHHS Approved 07/25/2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-25: Order of Disciplinary Action Appeal — MDHHS Approved 7/25/2025
purpose: Define the steps an EMS clinician or Life Support Agency must take to appeal an Order of Disciplinary Action issued by the Professional Standards Review Organization committee and Medical Control Authority.

sections.title: Appeal Grounds and Submission
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-25: ODA Appeal — Appeal Request
points: An EMS clinician or LSA may request appeal of an ODA based on new evidence, procedural errors, fraud or misrepresentation, or improper application of protocols, statute, or administrative rules
The MCA must be notified in writing within seven business days of ODA receipt
The appeal must include a detailed explanation of the reasons for appeal and any relevant supporting documentation
Submitting a Request to Appeal does not stay the provisions of the ODA

sections.title: MCA Initial Review and Assignment
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-25: ODA Appeal — MCA Procedure
points: The MCA Board chair and MCA staff review the appeal to determine whether it meets appeal requirements
If the appeal does not meet requirements, it must be returned with an explanation within three business days
The EMS clinician or LSA may resubmit within three business days with additional justification
New evidence or fraud/misrepresentation appeals are remanded to the PSRO committee that conducted the medical review
Procedural error appeals are referred to the MCA Board to determine whether the PSRO Investigation and Resolution protocol was violated
Improper application appeals are referred to the MCA Board for determination
Multiple appeal categories are referred to the MCA Board for assignment and resolution

sections.title: Appeal Meeting and Outcomes
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-25: ODA Appeal — Hearing and Outcome
points: If assigned to the MCA Board, the appeal meeting must be scheduled not less than three and not more than thirty business days after receiving the appeal request
The EMS clinician or LSA may request one postponement of up to thirty business days; the request must be received in writing within three business days before the meeting
During open session, the EMS clinician, LSA, or their designee may present appeal information while protecting PHI
The MCA Board may enter closed session to review protected information, PSRO decisions, grounds for appeal, protocol adherence, and ODA consistency
The MCA Board must return to open session and choose one outcome: affirm the ODA, revoke the ODA, diminish the ODA to a lesser action, or suspend the ODA
The final written decision must be provided within fourteen business days of the appeal meeting start date
If new information creates additional allegations, a new investigation must be conducted under the PSRO Complaint Investigation and Resolution process

sections.title: EMSCC Appeal
points: After exhausting the MCA appeal procedure, an EMS clinician or LSA may appeal to the State of Michigan Emergency Medical Services Coordination Committee under Part 209
EMSCC appeal determines whether the MCA action or decision followed Department-approved protocols, statute, or administrative rules
Specific facts of the underlying case leading to the ODA are not evaluated and must not be included in the EMSCC appeal request
The appeal must be filed in writing with MDHHS no more than 30 business days after written notification of the MCA's final determination
If appealed to EMSCC, the MCA must make available in writing the information it considered in making its decision

scope_notes: This protocol governs appeal of an ODA, not issuance of the ODA itself
Appeal grounds: new evidence, procedural error, fraud or misrepresentation, improper application
Appeal request due in writing within seven business days of ODA receipt
Appeal does not stay ODA provisions
Rejected appeal requests must be returned with explanation within three business days
Resubmission window after rejection: three business days
MCA Board appeal meeting window: not less than three and not more than thirty business days after receipt
Final MCA written decision due within fourteen business days of appeal meeting start
EMSCC appeal due within 30 business days after MCA final determination
